summary: `dedupe_oos.py` classifies each `[OOS]` conformance finding as one of three actions: `create` (no existing match → file a new scope ticket), `comment:<n>` (an open issue `<n>` already carries a matching embedded `<!-- dedup-key: <file/area>|<finding-type> -->` → post a comment instead of a duplicate ticket), or `suppress` (ruff/reformat-class noise or a within-run duplicate → drop silently). The dedup-key is `<file-or-area lowercased>|<finding-type>`; cross-run dedup matches it against `<!-- dedup-key: … -->` markers in open issue bodies, so every auto-filed scope ticket must embed its own dedup-key for later runs to find it. This replaces the older "one ticket per finding" behaviour. path:dark-factory/scripts/dedupe_oos.py
